def: s.m. Warding off, keeping off; hindering, stopping, obstructing; prevention, prohibition; advising (against), dissuading (from, syn. manʻ); — negation; refusal; denial; — discontinuance; contrariety (to), or deviation (from), rule; an exception; — inauspiciousness, evil
subs: v.t. To hinder, prevent, prohibit; to dissuade, &c. (i.q. manʻ karnā)

def: adj. (f. -ā), Keeping back (from), obstructing, preventing, prohibiting, prohibitive; forbidding; — negative; privative.

s.f. A pleonastic form ( = nishedh, q.v.). s.m. Warding off, keeping off; hindering, stopping, obstructing; prevention, prohibition; advising (against), dissuading (from, syn. manʻ); — negation; refusal; denial; — discontinuance; contrariety (to), or deviation (from), rule; an exception; — inauspiciousness, evil
subs: v.t. To hinder, prevent, prohibit; to dissuade, &c. (i.q. manʻ karnā)

def: adj. Obstructing, obstructive; preventive; prohibitive; — negative, privative; — inauspicious, evil, bad.
part. (f. -ā), Warded off; prevented, prohibited, forbidden; denied.
s.m. Sprinkling, infusion, aspersion;-irrigation; — distilling; — effusion; — seminal infusion, impregnation; — the ceremony performed upon the occasion of conception.
s.f. Sitting-place; resting-place; a bird's-nest.

ety: nishī, fr. nishī-dan, a var. form of nishastan+Zand man; S. मन्
def: s.f. Sitting-place; resting-place; seat, bench, stool; — a nest; — residence, mansion; settlement; one's native country; — the hams, the buttocks.
part. (f. -ā), Prohibited, forbidden; denied; kept back, restrained; warded off, prevented.
s.f. Prohibitedness; prohibition; prevention.

(imperat. & rt. of nishastan; see nishast), part. adj. & n. of ag. Sitting; seated; dwelling (in); settled or fixed (in); — sitter, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. gosha-nishīn, 'sitting in a corner,' 'solitary'; 'a recluse').
s.f. Sitting, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. gosha-nishīnī, 'retirement, seclusion,' &c.).
s.m. A weight of gold of one hundred and eight rattis.
adj. See the pop. form niskalank.
adj. = nish-kalaṅk.
adj. = nish-kalaṅk.
adj. (f. -ā), Free from wish or desire, disinterested, unselfish; — adv. Without wish or desire, unwillingly.
adj. (f. -ā), Free from thorns; free from ills, or trouble, or obstacles, or danger, or enemies; happy; plain, easy.
s.m. A species of tree, Capparis trifoliata.
adj. See the pop. form niskapaṭ.
s.f. Guilelessness, &c. (see nis-kapaṭ).
adj. & adv. Causeless, groundless, needless, unnecessary; — without motive or object, disinterested; — causelessly, &c.
s.m. Essence (of anything), gist, pith (syn. nićoṛ)
s.m. Issue, exit, egress (i.q. nikās); — a portico, a verandah.
adj. & adv. Belonging exclusively (to); — exclusively, solely; entirely, completely; merely, only.
adj. Without defect or blemish, unblemished; blameless, faultless; sound, &c. (see ni-khoṭ).
s.m. Going forth or out; — taking a child out of the house for the first time (an essential ceremony performed in the fourth month after birth, and accompanied by sacrifice).
s.m. Doing, effecting, accomplishing; concluding; producing, causiug, engendering.
part. (f. -ā), Done, effected; made, produced, prepared; achieved, accomplished.
adj. See the pop. form nir-paksh.
adj. = ni-spand, q.v. adj. Immovable; motionless, still; steady.
part. (f. -ā), Gone forth or out; sprung up, arisen, born, descended, produced, generated, brought about; effected, completed, concluded, accomplished, executed, done, finished, ready.
adj. (f. -ā, or -ī), Free from sin, sinless, guiltless, innocent.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Sinless, &c. (i.q. nish-pāp): — guiltless or innocent one.
adj. Destitute of power or prowess, powerless, weak.
s.f. Going forth or out; birth, production, generation; — completion, conclusion, termination, consummation.
s.m. A legume, a pod; a species of pulse, Phaseolus radiatus, or Dolichos sinensis; pulse (in general).
s.m. Striking, striking together, clashing; — the noise or sound produced by striking or clashing.
adj. Without fruit, bearing no fruit, fruitless, unfruitful, barren, unproductive; useless, unprofitable, inefficacious; — seedless, impotent.
s.f. A woman past child-bearing, or in whom menstruation has ceased.
s.f. = S نشپهلتو निष्फलत्व nish-phalatva, s.m. Unfruitfulness, barrenness, unproductiveness; unprofitableness; inefficacy; uselessness.
s.m. = S نشپهلتا निष्फलता nish-phalatā, s.f. Unfruitfulness, barrenness, unproductiveness; unprofitableness; inefficacy; uselessness.
s.f. = nish-phalā, q.v. s.f. A woman past child-bearing, or in whom menstruation has ceased.
adj. (f. -ā), Deprived of light or radiance; lustreless; gloomy, dark, obscure.
adj. Powerless.
s.f. = S نشپربهاوتو निष्प्रभावत्व nish-prabhāvatva, s.m. Powerlessness.
s.m. = ? نشپربهاوتا निष्प्रभावता nish-prabhāvatā, s.f. Powerlessness.
adj. Without authority, wanting authority; unauthorized; unauthenticated.
adj. Without authority, wanting authority; unauthorized; unauthenticated.
adj. Not influenced by any motive; without motives; causeless, groundless, reasonless; absolute; — useless, objectless; needless, unnecessary, uncalled for; — adv. Causelessly; unnecessarily, &c.
adj. = ni-spr̤ih, q.v.

adj. Deprived of men, without males; unpeopled, desolate; — not having produced male children, without male issue; — not male, feminine; — devoid of manliness, effeminate; — s.m. An impotent man; a eunuch; an effeminate man; a coward.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Sonless, childless; — a sonless person.
s.f. A lancet, fleam
subs: or nashtar lagānā (-ko, or -meṅ), To apply the lancet (to), to lance; to bleed, phlebotomize.

def: s.f. Station, position, standing; condition, state; stand-point; basis, foundation; — belief (in), faith; — conclusion, end, termination, catastrophe; accomplishment, completion; culminating point; disappearance, destruction, death; — fixed or certain knowledge, ascertainment, confirmation, certainty
subs: nishṭhā-vant, or nishṭhā-vān, adj. & s.m. Possessed of complete knowledge, perfect, complete, consummate; — fulfilling all religious duties; — one who is possessed of complete knowledge, &c.

def: s.m. Sauce; condiment.
adj. (f. -ā), Hard, rough; coarse; gruff, severe, austere, hard-hearted, pitiless, cruel; — contumelious (in speech).
s.f. = S نشٿهرتو निष्ठुरत्व ni-shṭhuratva, s.m. Harshness (of speech), coarseness, reviling, abuse; — severity, cruelty.
s.m. = S نشٿهرتا निष्ठुरता ni-shṭhuratā, s.f. Harshness (of speech), coarseness, reviling, abuse; — severity, cruelty.
(loc. of niś), In the night
subs: See s.v. niśā. s.f. Night (see niśā)
or nisi-bāsar, adv. Day and night; without intermission; continually
or nisā-ćar, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Going or prowling about by night; — an animal that prowls about and feeds by night; a night-prowler; a demon, goblin; a robber, thief
(˚āʼī = S. tā +ikā), s.f. Going or prowling about by night
s.f. A female fiend; a woman who keeps an assignation, a harlot
nisā-pati, or nis-pati, s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon
(i.q. S. niśeśa, i.e. niśā+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon.

def: adv. See nis-bāsar: &c., s.v. nis
subs: adj. & s.m. = niś-ćar, or niśā-ćar, q.v.

def: adv. See s.v. نش niśi, and nis-bāsar. adv. See nis-bāsar: &c., s.v. nis
subs: adj. & s.m. = niś-ćar, or niśā-ćar, q.v.

def: adj. & s.m. = niśā-ćar, q.v. adj. & s.m. See s.v. niśā (see also nis or nisā, and niśi). s.f. Night (see niśā)
subs: or nisi-bāsar, adv. Day and night; without intermission; continually
or nisā-ćar, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Going or prowling about by night; — an animal that prowls about and feeds by night; a night-prowler; a demon, goblin; a robber, thief
(˚āʼī = S. tā +ikā), s.f. Going or prowling about by night
s.f. A female fiend; a woman who keeps an assignation, a harlot
nisā-pati, or nis-pati, s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon
(i.q. S. niśeśa, i.e. niśā+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon.

def: adj. Having no boundaries or limits, unbounded, boundless, immeasurable.
ex: نفرت اور محبت کی اس کشاکش کو دیکھنا ہے تو ایک گُل کو دیکھو جس کے ابھرنے اور کھلنے میں نسیم کی عطر بیزی کے ساتھ سورج کی جاں فزائی بھی شامل ہے۔
If you wish to see the perplexity of love and hate, then observe a flower, for in its rising and blooming involves the fragrant play of the breeze and the life-giving warmth of the sun.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَسِیم (nasīm), borrowed from Arabic نَسِيم (nasīm). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi نسیم (nsym /⁠nasīm⁠/).

def: s.m. = nisindhu, q.v. s.m. A species of shrub, Vitex negundo.
s.m. A species of shrub, Vitex negundo.
s.f. See s.v. niśā. s.f. Night (see niśā)
subs: or nisi-bāsar, adv. Day and night; without intermission; continually
or nisā-ćar, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Going or prowling about by night; — an animal that prowls about and feeds by night; a night-prowler; a demon, goblin; a robber, thief
(˚āʼī = S. tā +ikā), s.f. Going or prowling about by night
s.f. A female fiend; a woman who keeps an assignation, a harlot
nisā-pati, or nis-pati, s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon
(i.q. S. niśeśa, i.e. niśā+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of the night,' the moon.

def: part. (f. -ā), Sharpened, whetted; ground; pointed; — (S. ni-śitam) s.m. Iron.
s.m. = نشيتهہ niśīth, q.v.
s.m. Midnight; night.
adj. Without stain, or flaw, or blemish; stainless, spotless, sinless, immaculate; unblemished; sound.
adj. Without fraud or deceit, guileless, artless, honest, sincere, candid, open.
s.f. Linseed, Linum usitatis simum.

ety: prob. S. निसृतः, or निःस्त्रुतः p.p.p. of rt. सृ, or स्त्रु, with निस्
def: adj. (dialec.) Pure, clear; unalloyed, &c. (i.q. nirā; nirālā, qq.v.); — (S. ni-sr̤itā), s.f. A species of purgative root, Indian jalap, Ipomæa turpethum.
adj. Immovable; motionless, still; steady.
adj. (f. -ā), Free from desire (for), not coveting, unenvious, content; — indifferent (to), disregarding.
adj. corr. of ni-spr̤ih, q.v.
s.f. A contented woman; — the plant Gloriosa superba.
adj. (f. -inī or -in) = ni-spr̤ih, q.v.
s.f. See the pop. form nisenī or nisainī.
s.m. Final beatitude (i.e. release of the soul from the necessity for further transmigration and its reunion with the Supreme Spirit), ultimate bliss or supreme felicity in the life to come.
adj. Unable to bear or support, or resist; impatient; — powerless, weak; — intolerable, unbearable irresistible.

adj. Free from uncertainty or doubt; undoubted, certain; — free from care or anxiety (syn. ni-sank); — adv. Without any doubt, undoubtedly, doubtless, certainly; — without hesitation or apprehension, freely.

ety: Prk. निस्संको, and निसंकु; S. निःशङ्कः, and निःशङ्कं
def: adj. & adv. Free from fear, or doubt, or suspicion; fearless, bold, daring; — without misgiving or apprehension; having no cares; free; — fearlessly, without care or anxiety, unhesitatingly.
adj. Destitute of offspring, without issue, childless.
adj. Without strength, or power, or vigour; without courage; unenergetic; powerless, impotent, weak; insignificant, low, mean; unsubstantial; — impoverished
subs: v.t. To deprive of power, or vigour, or energy; — to exhaust, to impoverish (soil).

def: s.m. Crossing, passing over or across; getting rid of, or away from; liberation, release, escape, acquittal; remission (of sins), final emancipation, salvation, beatitude; — blessing; — payment, discharge (of a debt), acquittance, definitive settlement, adjustment; requital; decision, decree
subs: s.m. The means of crossing (the ocean of life), a cause of final liberation; faith in any deity
v.t. To liberate, release, acquit; — to exempt (the soul) from further transmigration; — to discharge (a debt, &c.), to pay; to requite (i.q. nistārnā).

def: s.m. Crossing, passing over or across; getting rid of, or away from; liberation, release, escape, acquittal; remission (of sins), final emancipation, salvation, beatitude; — blessing; — payment, discharge (of a debt), acquittance, definitive settlement, adjustment; requital; decision, decree
subs: s.m. The means of crossing (the ocean of life), a cause of final liberation; faith in any deity
v.t. To liberate, release, acquit; — to exempt (the soul) from further transmigration; — to discharge (a debt, &c.), to pay; to requite (i.q. nistārnā).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Rescuing, delivering, releasing, saving; — one who effects the delivery or salvation (of), rescuer, deliverer, preserver, saviour.

ety: nistār˚ = S. निस्तारय(ति), caus. of rt. निस्+तॄ
def: v.t. To liberate, release, acquit; to rescue, save, deliver; — to exempt (the soul) from further transmigration, to beatify; — to discharge, pay off (a debt, &c.), to requite.
adj. Lacking strength or energy; destitute of fire, or heat, or lustre, &c.; powerless, impotent; spiritless, dull, tame; — lustreless, dim, obscure.
adj. Lacking strength or energy; destitute of fire, or heat, or lustre, &c.; powerless, impotent; spiritless, dull, tame; — lustreless, dim, obscure.
s.m. Definitive settle ment, adjustment, decision, decree (syn. ćukautā; nipṭerā); — adv. Wholly, totally, in toto, altogether, thoroughly, completely.
(for nis-tukh), adj. corr. of nis-tush, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Freed from chaff or husk; — (met.) purified; cleansed; simplified.
adj. (f. -ā), Freed from chaff or husk; — (met.) purified; cleansed; simplified.
adj. (rustic; — f. -ī), Careless, heedless; negligent; idle, lazy.
adj. Joyless, sad, unhappy; disagreeable, distressing.
adj. (f. -ā), Deprived of one's own; having no property; indigent, poor.
adj. Void of flavour, taste less, insipid, unsavoury; not sweet
s.m. = ni-sās, q.v. s.m. Breathing out, expiration; respiration; breath; — sighing, a sigh.
s.m. See the pop. form nisās.

ety: fr. A. nisyuṉ, or nasyuṉ; — nisyuṉ = nisyān, q.v.
def: s.m. A thing forgotten; a thing unworthy to be remembered, or of no regard or account; a thing dropped or thrown away as useless.
s.m. For getting; — forgetfulness; oblivion.
s.f. Forgetfulness (i.q. nisyān).

ety: Prk. नित्तो, नित्तं; S. नित्यः, नित्यं
def: adj. & adv. Continual, incessant, perpetual, repeated, constant, invariable, eternal, endless, &c. (see nitya); — always, ever, daily, continually, unceasingly, perpetually, constantly, invariably; — s.m. The base of an obtuse-angled triangle
subs: or nit-nit, adv. intens. of nit, q.v.
s.m., or nit-kiriyā, s.f. Daily duty, or routine; constant or indispensable act or duty (as observance of the five great sacrifices, or any daily and necessary rite)
or nit-nayā, adj. Ever new, or fresh; — changeable, variable.

def: s.f. = نيتی nīti, q.v. s.f. Guidance, direction, management; — manner of conducting oneself, manners, conduct, propriety; right, or moral, or prudent behaviour; morality; prudence; — moral philosophy, ethics; — prudent counsel, policy; polity, political wisdom or science; political economy; state policy, statesmanship
subs: vulg. nītigya, adj. & s.m. Knowing what is right in polity and in morals; sagacious, politic, prudent; — a moralist; a prudent statesman or soldier; a politician
vulg. nīt-śāstar or sāstar, s.m. The science of ethics, moral science; political science; — a treatise on the science of ethics, or of politics
s.f. Any treatise on moral or political science, or on good government; a discourse on political economy; — a moral tale

or nīti-mant, or nīti-mān, adj. & s.m. (f. -matī), Knowing the rules of moral or prudent behaviour; moral; prudent; eminent for political wisdom; — one who knows the rules of moral or prudent behaviour, a moral person, &c.
vulg. nīt-bidyā, s.f. Moral or political science, political economy
s.f. The application or employment of prudence, or good policy, &c. (see nīti).

def: adj. (dialec.) Straight, direct; — upright, straightforward (as conduct); right, proper, fit, suitable.
adv. contrac. of nimitt, q.v.
(dialec.), 1˚ corr. of nit, or nitya, q.v.; — 2˚ contrac. of nīṭ, q.v.
s.m.(?) A sheet of dressed leather (which is spread as a table-cloth, or upon which people play chess, or draughts, &c.).

adj. & adv. (dialec.) = nitya, or nit, qq.v. adj. & adv. Continual, perpetual, constant, uninterrupted, incessant, endless, eternal; invariable, regular, fixed; necessary, obligatory, essential; — continually, perpetually, &c.; always, ever, daily, at all times, for ever (see nit)
subs: (˚ya+an˚), adj. (f. -ā), Eternal and perishable; permanent and temporary

(˚ya+an˚), s.m. Invariable, or obligatory, suspension of the repetition of the Vedas (a term applied to periods when the perusal of the Vedas is prohibited, as the day of full moon, new moon, the eighth and fourteenth days of the half month)
(˚ya+ān˚), s.m. Eternal happiness; — adj. & s.m. Always happy
s.m. The constant dissolution of living beings
adv. Always, ever, at all times, perpetually, constantly, &c. (i.q. nitya)
s.m. Constantly keeping, or protecting, or preserving
adj. Of eternal duration, imperishable; ineradicable
s.m., or nitya-kriyā, s.f. Constant act or duty, regular and necessary act or ceremony (as observance of the five great sacrifices, or any daily and necessary rite)
adj. (f. -ī), Formed, or consisting, of anything eternal; durable; eternal
adj. (f. -ā), Always busy, much occupied, ever engaged (in)
s.m. Perpetual youth; — adj. (f. -ī, or -ā), Ever or always young.

def: adj. Of or relating to conduct, or manners, or policy, &c. (see nīti); for the guidance of conduct, &c.; — ethical, moral; political.
s.m. Name of one of the seven divisions of the lower regions (pātāl).
s.m. Rump; buttocks, posteriors (esp. of a woman); — ridge, side, slope, or protuberant flank (of a mountain); — sloping bank, slope (of a river); gibbosity.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Having large buttocks; having beautiful hips; — s.m. A person with large buttocks, or fine hips
subs: s.f. A woman with large and handsome hips.

def: adj. & adv. Much, considerable, excessive, extraordinary; — exceedingly, excessively, very.
adj. (dialec. or rustic) Poor; worn-out; exhausted (as land that has been over-cultivated).
s.m. = nipṭārā, q.v. s.m. Determination, settlement, termination, finishing, &c. (i.q. nibṭerā, nibṭāʼo, nibeṛā, qq.v.).

ety: fr. ni with the compar. aff. tar
def: adv. Wholly, entirely; much, extremely, excessively; always, continually, eternally.

v.t. (dialec.) = نتهارنا nithārnā, q.v. v.t. To make clear or clean, to purify (water, or other liquid, by letting the feculent matter subside and pouring off the clear water, &c.); to pour off; to draw off (an infusion, &c.).
adv. (dialec.) Scarcely; hardly
s.f. Basis; certain knowledge; confirmation, &c. (see nishṭhā).
adj. Out of work or employment, unemployed; idle; — s.m. Want of employment; idleness.

ety: nithār˚ = Prk. नित्थाल(इ), or नित्थाले(इ) = S. निस्थलय(ति), caus. of नि+स्थल्; or = S. निस्तारय(ति), caus. of rt. निस्+तॄ
def: v.t. To make clear or clean, to purify (water, or other liquid, by letting the feculent matter subside and pouring off the clear water, &c.); to pour off; to draw off (an infusion, &c.).
v.n. To be made clear or clean, to be purified (water or other liquid, by letting the feculent matter subside, and pouring off the clear liquid).
part. adj. (f. -ī), Made clear or clean, purified (water, &c., the impurity of which has subsided); clear, clean, pure (water, or other liquid)

ety: Prk. निट्ठुरो or निट्ठुलो; S. निष्ठुरः
def: adj. Harsh, severe, cruel, unfeeling; coarse; contumelious (as speech); hard, obdurate, relentless; — sly, cunning, shrewd.

ety: niṭhur + S. ता; — āʼī = ˚tāʼī = S. tā+ikā
def: s.f. Harshness (of speech, &c.), severity, coarseness, reviling, abuse; hardness of heart, cruelty; obduracy, relentlessness; stubbornness, wilfulness.

ety: niṭhur + S. ता; — āʼī = ˚tāʼī = S. tā+ikā
def: s.f. Harshness (of speech, &c.), severity, coarseness, reviling, abuse; hardness of heart, cruelty; obduracy, relentlessness; stubbornness, wilfulness.

ety: niṭhur + S. ता; — āʼī = ˚tāʼī = S. tā+ikā
def: s.f. Harshness (of speech, &c.), severity, coarseness, reviling, abuse; hardness of heart, cruelty; obduracy, relentlessness; stubbornness, wilfulness.

s.f. Guidance, direction, management; — manner of conducting oneself, manners, conduct, propriety; right, or moral, or prudent behaviour; morality; prudence; — moral philosophy, ethics; — prudent counsel, policy; polity, political wisdom or science; political economy; state policy, statesmanship
subs: vulg. nītigya, adj. & s.m. Knowing what is right in polity and in morals; sagacious, politic, prudent; — a moralist; a prudent statesman or soldier; a politician
vulg. nīt-śāstar or sāstar, s.m. The science of ethics, moral science; political science; — a treatise on the science of ethics, or of politics
s.f. Any treatise on moral or political science, or on good government; a discourse on political economy; — a moral tale

or nīti-mant, or nīti-mān, adj. & s.m. (f. -matī), Knowing the rules of moral or prudent behaviour; moral; prudent; eminent for political wisdom; — one who knows the rules of moral or prudent behaviour, a moral person, &c.
vulg. nīt-bidyā, s.f. Moral or political science, political economy
s.f. The application or employment of prudence, or good policy, &c. (see nīti).

def: s.f. Invariableness; perpetuity, eternity, &c. (see nit).
s.f. corr. of niktī, q.v. s.f. A small balance or scales (such as goldsmiths and bankers use).

adj. & adv. Continual, perpetual, constant, uninterrupted, incessant, endless, eternal; invariable, regular, fixed; necessary, obligatory, essential; — continually, perpetually, &c.; always, ever, daily, at all times, for ever (see nit)
subs: (˚ya+an˚), adj. (f. -ā), Eternal and perishable; permanent and temporary

(˚ya+an˚), s.m. Invariable, or obligatory, suspension of the repetition of the Vedas (a term applied to periods when the perusal of the Vedas is prohibited, as the day of full moon, new moon, the eighth and fourteenth days of the half month)
(˚ya+ān˚), s.m. Eternal happiness; — adj. & s.m. Always happy
s.m. The constant dissolution of living beings
adv. Always, ever, at all times, perpetually, constantly, &c. (i.q. nitya)
s.m. Constantly keeping, or protecting, or preserving
adj. Of eternal duration, imperishable; ineradicable
s.m., or nitya-kriyā, s.f. Constant act or duty, regular and necessary act or ceremony (as observance of the five great sacrifices, or any daily and necessary rite)
adj. (f. -ī), Formed, or consisting, of anything eternal; durable; eternal
adj. (f. -ā), Always busy, much occupied, ever engaged (in)
s.m. Perpetual youth; — adj. (f. -ī, or -ā), Ever or always young.

def: adv. See s.v. نتيه nitya. adj. & adv. Continual, perpetual, constant, uninterrupted, incessant, endless, eternal; invariable, regular, fixed; necessary, obligatory, essential; — continually, perpetually, &c.; always, ever, daily, at all times, for ever (see nit)

adj. Without spaces or interstices; close, contiguous; thick, gross, dense, impervious (as a forest, or darkness, &c.); — coarse; — heavy, hard (as rain, &c.); sound (sleep); — firm; — large, bulky; — crooked-nosed; — s.m. A crooked-nosed person; — name of a mountain.

def: s.f. Returning; return; — disappearing; disappearance; — ceasing; cessation; abstaining (from), abstinence; suspension (of); relaxation, inactivity; repose, rest, ease; — final emancipation; felicity, bliss, beatitude (in these senses corr. fr. nir-vr̤itti); — abolition; prevention; — completion
subs: s.m. The path of final emancipation, the way of salvation
s.m. One who pursues the path of emancipation
(nivr̤itti+ar˚), adv. For the sake of final emancipation.

adj. (old H.) = nij, q.v.

(f. -ā), Checked, curbed, restrained; controlled, governed, subdued; self-governed; — constituted; established, fixed, settled, constant, steady, permanent; appointed; prescribed; — ascertained, determined; confirmed; — determinate; given (as a line, or point, &c., in Geom.); certain, inevitable, destined; — positive, definite

adj. = nirādar, q.v.; — s.m. (colloq.) = an-ādar, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Without respect, unrespected, disrespected, despised, dishonoured, disgraced; — showing no respect, disrespectful; unceremonious, impolite; — s.m. Disrespect; contempt, scorn; insult

def: s.m. A sheath, scabbard, case (i.q. miyān, q.v.); — a bandage (for a broken limb), splint; — a plough-tail or handle.

s.m. Restraining, checking; preventing; — restraint; restriction, limitation; — fixing; defining; definition; — fixed rule or law, principle; necessity, obligation; rule, regulation, precept; custom, usage, practice; — certainty, ascertainment; — agreement, arrangement, contract, compact, bond, engagement, assent, promise, vow; — any self-imposed restraint or religious observance voluntarily practised (as fasting, watching, pilgrimage, praying, &c.); voluntary penance; supererogatory devotion or piety; — a religious observance (in general); temperateness; piety

adj. Regular; punctual; — religious, pious; true to (one's) engagements, or obligations, or vows; conscientious; abstemious, temperate; continent, ohaste.

part. (f. -ā), Checked, restrained; prevented; suppressed, bound, confined; — restricted or limited (to); — governed, guided; — fixed, established; regulated; prescribed, enjoined, appointed; — agreed upon, stipulated, covenanted.

One who extracts metals from their scoria; — one who buys the ashes and rubbish of a goldsmith's shop and sifts it to find gold, &c.; — one who washes and sifts the sand of a river or stream for gold, or silver, &c.; — one who sifts or washes the dust of thoroughfares to see what he can find; — adj. & s.m. Cautious, prudent, not easily imposed upon; — a cautious man; a sharp or clever fellow.

conj. & adv. Also, likewise; even; too; moreover; again.

(inf. n. of نَازَعَ 'to dispute, contend,' &c., iii of نزع 'to hasten (to); to retire,' &c.), s.m. & f. Dispute, contention, altercation, controversy; litigation, law-suit; — the subject of dispute, the point at issue (i.q. mā bi-hiʼn nizāʻ)

ety: for A. نظامة, fr. niz̤ām, q.v.
def: s.f. Arrangement, regulation, government, administration of justice; — the office of nāz̤im

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نظامت (nizāmat), from Arabic نِظامَة (niẓāma).

ety: prob. zār, q.v. with prefix ni

adj. One's own; private.

def: adj. Altered or changed (by grief or age); sad, sorrowful, cast down, dejected, drooping; — angry, wrathful, raging, distracted; — formidable, terrible; low (ground, &c.).
s.m. A scratch, claw; a pinch
subs: or noćā-khasoṭī, s.f. Mutual scratching and tearing
To scratch and claw (or tear, or pinch) each other.

ety: noć˚ = Prk. निउंच(इ) = S. निकुञ्च(ति), rt. नि+कुञ्च्
def: v.t. To pinch, to grip; to scratch, claw; — to pull out, pluck out (these meanings point to the S. lunć as their source); — to fleece (a person).
s.m. A pincher, &c.; — one who fleeces (another), an extortioner; — one who purloins, or embezzles.
s.f. (dialec.) = nawī, q.v. s.f. The string with which a cow's legs are tied when milking (i.q. naunā; nainā); a tether.

s.f. The tip of the tongue

def: adj. contrac. of anokhā, q.v.

s.f. Efflorescence of salt (on a wall, &c.).

s.m. A drink, a draught; anything drunk (esp. sweet, agreeable, and wholesome); — a present; — a reward; — an antidote; — part. adj. & s.m. (fr. nosh-īdan, a denom. fr. nosh) Drinking; — drinker (used as last member of compounds, e.g. bāda-nosh, 'wine-drinker')

s.f. An antidote; a remedy; — treacle; — an electuary

To drink, eat, devour, swallow, sup, sip.

def: adj. Sweet; cheerful; — fortunate, prosperous, happy.

To drink, eat, devour, swallow, sup, sip.

ety: nosh, q.v.+īn = Zend aêna; S. इन्
def: adj. Sweet, pleasant, agreeable; — easy of digestion.
s.m. A currency-note; bank-note; cheque; draft (also written loṭ)
s.m. = nr̤i-varāh, q.v.
s.m. 'Man-god,' a god among men, a king.
s.m. A manly property, or duty.
s.m. A protector of men; a prince, king, sovereign; a warrior; (in Poetry) a symbol for the number sixteen
subs: (˚pa+ābh˚), s.m. Music played at the royal meals
vulg. nr̤ipātmaj (˚pa+āt˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Of royal birth; — a king's son, a prince
(˚pa+ās˚), s.m. 'Seat of a king'; a throne, a chair of state
(˚pa+ām˚), s.m. 'King's disease,' consumption; the king's evil(?)
(˚pa+aṉ˚), s.m. King's share, royal portion (i.e. a sixth, eighth, or twelfth, of grain; a sixth of fruit; a fiftieth of merchandize, &c.), royal revenue
(˚pa+āv˚), s.m. A kind of gem (i.q. rājāvart)
s.m. A thorny species of bamboo, Bambusa spinosa; — a variety of Saccharum sara; — the mango tree
s.m. (f. -ā), Son of a king, a prince
s.m. (f. -ā), A king's son, a prince
s.m. (f.-ā), 'Rejoicer of a king,' a king's son, a prince
vulg. nirp-nīt, s.f. Regal polity; king-craft; rules of conduct and government for a king
adj. (f. -wālī), Of or pertaining to a sovereign
(˚pa+uć˚), adj. Suited to a king, kingly, princely.

def: s.m. See s.v. nr̤ipa or nr̤ip.
s.m. 'Protector of men,' a king, monarch.
s.m. See s.v. nr̤ipa or nr̤ip.
s.f. = S نرپتو नृपत्व nr̤ipatva, s.m. Royalty, kingship; sovereignty, dominion.
s.m. 'Lord of men,' a king, a prince.
s.m. = S نرپتا नृपता nr̤ipatā, s.f. Royalty, kingship; sovereignty, dominion.
adj. See s.v. nr̤ipa or nr̤ip.
s.f. Dancing; acting, &c. (i.q. nr̤itya, q.v.).
s.m. A dancer; an actor; a mime.
s.m. The science, or the practice, of dancing; dancing; dance; acting; gesticulation, pantomime; — jumping about, capering, skipping
subs: s.f. A dancing-room; a play-house, a theatre (syn. nāć-ghar)

def: s.m. (f. -ikā, or -ī), A dancer, &c. (i.q. nartak, q.v.). s.m. (f. -ī), Dancer; actor, mime, mummer, gesticulator; player (of any description); juggler; — a dancing-master.
s.f. A female dancer, &c. (i.q. nartakī, q.v.). s.f. A female dancer; an actress; a nāć-girl, singing-girl.
s.f. A female dancer, &c. (i.q. nartakī, q.v.). s.f. A female dancer; an actress; a nāć-girl, singing-girl.
s.m. 'The man-boar,' Vishṇu in his boar-incarnation (with the figure of a man and the head of a wild boar).

ety: for A. نبوة, abst. s. fr. نبؤ, inf. n. of نبأ 'to announce,' &c.
def: s.f. Prophecy; divining
v.n. To be scratched, or clawed, or pinched; to be plucked or pulled; — v.t. = noćnā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to be scratched, or pinched, &c.; — to cause to scratch, or pinch, &c.
s.m. The mulberry tree, Morus indica.
s.m. pl. (of nadīm, q.v.), Companions (of a king); courtiers.
s.m. (dialec.) A species of tobacco.

ety: for A. ندرة, abst. s. fr. ندر 'to remain alone'; cf. nādir
def: s.f. Soleness; solitariness (syn. tanhāʼī); oddness, singularity, rareness.

ety: inf. n. of نفر 'to run away in terror'
def: adj. Frightened, terrified, scared; fleeing (from); — averse (to); abhorring.
s.m. pl. (of nafs), Souls; spirits; selves; individuals, persons.
s.m. = nafz̤, and nafāz̤, qq.v. s.m. Penetrating, pervading, piercing through (i.q. nafāz̤).

ety: fr. the Hebrew; rt. נוח 'to rest; be comforted,' &c.
def: prop. n. The patriarch Noah.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نوح (nūḥ), from Arabic نُوح (nūḥ).

def: adj. Nine.
ex: میرا نام بہت لمبا ہے، نہ؟
merā nām bahut lambā hai, na?
My name is very long, isn't it?
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀡 (ṇa), from Sanskrit न (na), likely influenced by Classical Persian نه (na). Doublet of نا (nā).

def: s.m. A scratch with the nail or talon, a claw
subs: v.t. To scratch with the nails or claws.

ety: old P. navama; Pehl. nāhum; Zend nāuma; S. नवम
def: adj. Ninth.

ety: for A. نحوسة, inf. n. of نحُس 'to be unlucky'
def: s.f. Inauspiciousness; misfortune, evil; poverty; — a bad presage; an unhappy accident.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نحوست (nuhūsat), from Arabic نُحُوسَة (nuḥūsa).

def: s.m. pl. (of najīb, q.v.), The noble; the excellent, &c.; nobles; heroes.
adj. & s.m. Astronomical; astrological; — an astronomer; astrologer (syn. jotishī).
s.f. (dialec.) The astronomical, or astrological, art or science.
s.m. pl. (of naqīb, q.v.), Princes, chiefs, leaders, tribunes.
adj. Having no points or dots (a letter); — insulting, abusive, scurrilous, foul.
s.m. Chick-pea, 'gram,' Cicer arietinum (syn. ćanā)
subs: vulg. naḵẖūd-ābā, and naḵẖd-āwā, s.m. 'Vetch water,' a decoction of meat with vetches (used after physic has been taken; or in certain diseases).

def: s.m. Beginning; principle; — the first; — adv. At first
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نُخُسْت (nuxust). Compare روزِ نُخُسْت (roz-i nuxust) and Hindi रोज़-ए-नुख़ुस्त (roz-e-nuxust).

def: s.f. First-fruits; — priority.

ety: nuḵẖust+īn = Zend yaṅh = S. ईयान् (base ईयस्)
def: adj. & adv. The first, or the very first; — firstly, in the first place.

ety: nukk(a) = nok, q.v.+Prk. इल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Having a sharp point; pointed; — tapering; — (in Bot.) acute; — (met.) charming, fascinating.
s.m. (dialec.) A pointed stick
subs: or nukkā mārnā (-ko), To strike or goad with a pointed stick; — to attack successfully; — to put a spoke in one's wheel; — to have carnal intercourse with (a woman).
